channel.title: The Back Office by Clara
channel.description: Field notes on running a growing business — the calls, the customers, the things nobody tells you.

item.title: Meta Just Put $115M Behind the Trades
item.description: Meta's America's Workforce Academy is the largest private-sector commitment to the skilled trades in US history. Free training, guaranteed jobs, and a clear signal: the trades are the future.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: Mark Roberts: How a Welsh Plumber Stopped Losing Work to Voicemail
item.description: Mark runs Roberts Plumbing in Wales with two junior plumbers. Watch how Clara changed the way he handles calls — and got his evenings back.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: What Is an IVR System and Is AI Replacing It?
item.description: IVR stands for Interactive Voice Response. It's the menu system you hear when you call a business. Here's how it works, why it frustrates callers, and what conversational AI is doing differently.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: Excluded Contacts: choose which calls to answer yourself
item.description: You can now choose specific contacts that Clara will never answer - your partner, family, or that supplier you need to speak to yourself. Set it up in under a minute.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: The 5-Minute Rule: Why 78% of Trades Jobs Go to the First Person Who Responds
item.description: The data is pretty clear: most enquiries come in after hours, and the first tradesperson to respond wins the job most of the time. Here's what that means in practice.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: The Tradesperson's Guide to Getting More Google Reviews (Without Being Weird About It)
item.description: Most tradespeople know reviews matter. Most of them hate asking for them. Here's how to do it without feeling like you're begging - and what your phone manner has to do with it.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: Why Dog Groomers, Mobile Vets and Pet Sitters Are Drowning in Missed Bookings
item.description: Pet services is one of the fastest-growing sectors in small business. It's also one where the gap between enquiry and booking loses more jobs than almost anything else.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: What It's Actually Like Running a Trade Business as a Woman in 2025
item.description: The challenges solo female operators face in trades are specific - and mostly not the ones people assume. A few honest observations from the people living it.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: Why Your Best Customers Keep Calling Back (And How to Make Sure They Do)
item.description: Repeat business in trades and service work isn't really about loyalty programmes or follow-up emails. It's about whether people felt like they could trust you - and a lot of that gets decided before the job even starts.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: How to invoice as a tradesperson and get paid in 7 days (free template)
item.description: The 9 things every tradesperson invoice needs, the line that cuts late payment by half, and how to send invoices in 2 minutes from site. With a free invoice generator built for trades.
item.author: Kir Sidorets

item.title: What to put on a job sheet (with a free template for any trade)
item.description: The 8 fields every tradesperson job sheet needs, why customer signatures matter on every job, and how to capture them on a phone in 2 minutes - with a free trade-specific template.
item.author: Kir Sidorets

item.title: What Tradespeople Charge Per Hour in 2026 - Real Hourly Rates by Trade and Region
item.description: Real 2026 hourly rate data for UK, US, and Canadian tradespeople - plumbers, electricians, HVAC, carpenters, gardeners, cleaners. By experience, by region, with emergency uplifts.
item.author: Kir Sidorets

item.title: What is an EICR - and how much does one cost in 2026?
item.description: Plain-English explainer on EICR (Electrical Installation Condition Report) - what it covers, who legally needs one, what it costs in the UK in 2026, and how to find a registered electrician quickly.
item.author: Kir Sidorets

item.title: 50 Professional Voicemail Greeting Examples for Canadian Businesses (2026)
item.description: Real voicemail greeting scripts for Canadian trades, dentists, real estate, salons, and more — standard hours, after-hours, holiday, and out-of-office. Free generator included.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: 50 Professional Voicemail Greeting Examples for UK Businesses (2026)
item.description: Real voicemail greeting scripts for UK trades, dentists, estate agents, salons, and more — standard hours, after-hours, holiday, and out-of-office. Free generator included.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: 50 Professional Voicemail Greeting Examples for US Businesses (2026)
item.description: Real voicemail greeting scripts for US trades, dentists, real estate, salons, and more — standard hours, after-hours, holiday, and out-of-office. Free generator included.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: 5 Best AI Phone Answering Services for Canadian Contractors 2026
item.description: 5 AI phone answering services compared for Canadian contractors and home service businesses. Real pricing, what each does well, and which fits solo vs growing crews.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: 5 Best AI Phone Answering Services for UK Tradesmen 2026
item.description: 5 AI phone answering services compared for self-employed UK tradesmen. Pricing from £49.99/mo, what each does well, and which fits sole traders vs growing firms.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: 5 Best AI Phone Answering Services for US Contractors 2026
item.description: 5 AI phone answering services compared for US contractors and home service businesses. Real pricing, what each does well, and which fits solo vs growing crews.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: All calls, answered: the new way Clara works
item.description: We've changed how Clara handles incoming calls. Instead of only catching the ones you miss, Clara can now be your primary answering service - with scheduling, rules, and call learning built in.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: How Carpenters and Joiners Stop Missing Jobs While They're on the Tools
item.description: Carpenters can't answer the phone mid-cut. An AI receptionist captures enquiry details - what the job is, where, and how to reach them - so you follow up with everything you need.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: Why Taxi and Private Hire Operators Are Switching to AI Receptionists
item.description: Missed calls mean lost bookings in taxi and private hire. AI receptionists answer 24/7, capture pickup details, and make sure every job reaches the driver - even at 2am.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: The calls you miss at 6pm are worth more than the ones at 10am
item.description: After-hours calls aren't just inconvenient - they're disproportionately high-intent. Here's why the evening is where most small businesses leak the most money.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: How mobile mechanics are winning jobs from the driveway
item.description: Mobile mechanics can't answer the phone while they're under a car. But the ones who are growing fastest have figured out what to do about it.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: Why heating engineers lose more leads in April than December
item.description: Winter emergencies keep the phone ringing. Spring servicing season is where the real lead loss happens - and most heating engineers don't see it coming.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: How roofers handle enquiries when they're on a job
item.description: When you're working at height, answering the phone isn't just inconvenient - it's dangerous. Here's how roofing businesses deal with the enquiry problem.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: "I don't want to talk to an AI"
item.description: The most common concern about Clara, and the answer isn't as simple as 'they'll be fine with it.'
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: Why electricians lose leads they never knew they had
item.description: The missed calls that hurt most are the ones you don't know about. For electricians, invisible lead loss is more common than most realise.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: What actually happens when an AI answers your business calls
item.description: AI receptionists are starting to replace voicemail for small businesses. Here's what the experience is really like, from the caller's side and the business owner's.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: Can't Siri already do this?
item.description: It's a fair question. But Siri and Clara are solving completely different problems.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: Taking on more cleaning clients without the chaos
item.description: Growing a cleaning business sounds simple. In practice, every new client adds more admin, more calls, and more things to keep track of.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: Why phone tag is killing dog grooming businesses
item.description: Dog grooming runs on repeat bookings and loyal clients. But the back-and-forth of scheduling is eating into time that should be spent on the actual work.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: No, it's not 'press 1 for sales'
item.description: When people hear 'AI phone system,' they picture the worst kind of automation. Clara isn't that.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: How estate agents are judged before the first viewing
item.description: Vendors choose their agent before they've seen a single property. The decision often comes down to one thing: how quickly you picked up the phone.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: Keeping your gardening business ticking over in the off-season
item.description: The winter slowdown doesn't have to mean a dry diary. How gardeners keep enquiries warm and clients loyal through the quieter months.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: Why the phone is the hardest part of running a removals company
item.description: Removals is a complex, high-stakes service to sell over the phone. Most companies handle it the same way - and most leave money on the table because of it.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: What it actually costs to answer your own phone
item.description: Most self-employed tradespeople think of the phone as free. It isn't. Here's what it's actually costing you.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: Why your busiest days are bad for future business
item.description: When the work is flowing, it's easy to feel like everything is fine. But your busiest days are often the ones quietly costing you the most.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: What customers think when nobody picks up
item.description: The missed call is only half the story. What happens in the customer's head after nobody answers tells you a lot about why they don't call back.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: The callback you keep meaning to make
item.description: It's on your list. It's been on your list for three days. At some point, it stopped being a lead and became a guilt trip.
item.author: Shani Sofer

item.title: What missed calls actually cost tradespeople (the numbers most never add up)
item.description: A locksmith handled 68 calls in his first month with Clara. 16 turned into booked jobs. Here's how to work out what unanswered calls are costing your business.
item.author: Adam Stevens

item.title: On being busy versus building something
item.description: Busy feels like progress. Sometimes it is. But there's a difference between a business that's growing and one that's just full.
item.author: Adam Stevens
